previous_majors_cleared: [2026-04-25 → carried by 2026-04-26 integrity 08:15] build_reproducibility CRITICAL — RESOLVED by decider 09-26 run (`integrity_note: "Build drift from 08:15Z integrity report is RESOLVED by this run — fresh build committed."`); confirmed by latest commit cd1ba07 09:40:56Z carrying refreshed docs/index.html

findings.description: Four git-owned files differ between FUSE workspace and origin/main: data/wins.json (+655 bytes WS), data/sections.json (+2984), build-scripts/generate-html.js (+899 WS but content drift, not just size — see evidence), docs/index.html (-303 WS). Both wins.json and sections.json have identical entry counts (71 wins, 0 sections per current schema with prose moved to sections.json:_meta) and identical {win_id, verdict} tuples — divergence is text-level only. Root cause: operator session pushed commit cd1ba07 'EXP-249 Phase B1: pie chart token-driven + label ink + 24x24 legend' at 2026-04-26T09:40:56Z without running `node build.js publish`, so git→FUSE propagation didn't happen. The decider 09:18Z run already committed/built (per its 09:26 daily report integrity_note); the operator's manual EXP-249 commit followed, leaving FUSE behind. Risk surface: integrity (next run 2026-04-27T08:07Z) and social (next run 2026-04-26T10:05Z, ~14m) read from FUSE without freshness protection. Both will see 4 stale files unless workspace-sync's 10:03Z run regenerates docs/index.html (per HNOTE-OPERATOR-UNIVERSAL-PUSHER-001 follow-up commit 0441ab3 at 08:33Z, workspace-sync now regenerates docs/index.html). For data/wins.json + data/sections.json + build-scripts/generate-html.js: workspace-sync is in NEVER_PUSH for build-scripts/, and per universal-pusher mode it pushes runtime data files only when FUSE is newer than git — here git is newer, so workspace-sync's mtime-guard correctly skips them. Self-heal path: any future operator `build.js publish` will fix all four; otherwise FUSE will stay stale until next operator session.
findings.evidence: md5 comparison FUSE vs raw.githubusercontent.com main: data/wins.json ws=1e7af841 gh=ae4f543f; data/sections.json ws=d8657bce gh=62b5e2b4; build-scripts/generate-html.js ws=ccc0c2a8 gh=edb7e8dc; docs/index.html ws=ca491204 gh=01ba9b5d. Sizes (bytes): wins.json ws=461704 gh=461049 (+655); sections.json ws=565490 gh=562506 (+2984); generate-html.js ws=143464 gh=142565 (+899); docs/index.html ws=1358172 gh=1358475 (-303). Workspace mtimes: wins/sections=09:28:41Z (decider rebuild), generate-html/docs=09:41:05Z (workspace-sync mid-flight at run start; not yet reflecting commit cd1ba07). Latest commits: cd1ba07 EXP-249 09:40:56Z, 6898fec workspace-sync 09:05:04Z, 0441ab3 'workspace-sync regenerates docs/index.html' 08:33:22Z.

suggested_fix.rationale: No-action this run. Three reasons: (a) workspace-sync's 10:03Z run will likely regenerate docs/index.html via its post-08:33 commit logic; (b) the 09:18Z decider already saw the divergence on its clean clone and produced a coherent build that the operator extended with EXP-249 — the canonical state is git, not FUSE; (c) the only agents that read from FUSE without protection (integrity + social) are not running until 27T08:07Z and ~10:05Z respectively, and even social only reads docs/llms.txt + data/ which are fine (llms.txt OK, wins.json/sections.json semantically identical at the entry level). If FUSE is still stale at the 11:18Z decider run, decider's clean-clone protection will absorb it. Re-check on the 2026-04-27 tinker run: if FUSE is still divergent, escalate to a manual `cp` from a clean clone.

findings.description: Decider 403 push-failure rate is 8 events in 16h (every ~2h, matching the 120m decider cron almost 1:1). Per HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001 (2026-04-25T18:24Z, status=consumed) the PAT genuinely has contents:write — the 403 is environmental/transient and the decider is supposed to retry once on 403 (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md retry-on-403 wrapper). Universal-pusher mode (HNOTE-OPERATOR-UNIVERSAL-PUSHER-001/002) is rescuing every event by syncing files to FUSE and letting workspace-sync push within ~1h. Net effect: data is reaching git, but every decider run pays the cost of a failed push + rescue ceremony, the integrity check failed once (08:15Z build_reproducibility CRITICAL), and the rescue creates a churn pattern (large workspace-sync commits) that operationally hides what changed. The 100% 403-rate is suspicious — if the retry-on-403 wrapper were working, at least some retries would succeed and the failure log would not be perfectly periodic. Possible root cause: the retry is firing but the same IP/session is also getting blocked on retry; alternatively the retry wrapper isn't actually firing (decider prompt says it does, but the failure logs don't reference any retry attempt).
findings.evidence: 8 push-failure files in monitor/integrity/: 2026-04-25T17-28, 19-30, 23-28, 2026-04-26T01-29, 03-27, 05-29, 06-42, 09-28. Latest content: '403 Permission denied to Devilwitch/Devilwench — scheduled task session IP. Known transient per HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001. Universal-pusher rescue executed.' No retry-attempt evidence in any of the 8 files. Decider runs at 17:28, 19:28, 21:28, 23:28, 01:28, 03:28, 05:28(?), 07:28(?), 09:28 — failure pattern lines up 1:1 with cron schedule (excluding the q4h slots that align to even hours where retry might mask). HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001.consumed_note: 'Decider self-apply procedure already updated (retry-on-403 wrapper per decider-patches-and-selfapply.md).'

suggested_fix.replace: verify the wrapper is actually being invoked and is logging its retry attempts. If it's emitting 'retrying after 30s' style log lines, those should appear in push-failure-*.json or in the daily-report's push_status block. Currently neither shows them. Either (a) the wrapper exists but isn't logging, in which case add a retry_attempts: N field to push-failure files; or (b) the wrapper isn't running at all in the current code path (e.g., catches push errors at a different layer).
suggested_fix.rationale: 100% failure rate after a documented retry implementation suggests the retry isn't reaching the GitHub push, or it is and the retry also fails. Diagnosing requires either verbose retry logging or a synthetic test from a fresh clone. Tinker can't audit decider's own internal push logic; this needs to be a PROP for the operator OR a decider self-investigation.

findings.fix_reason_held: Diagnosing why a retry wrapper isn't visibly retrying requires reading the decider's actual code path in decider-patches-and-selfapply.md and likely instrumentation work. Conservative tinker doesn't rewrite the decider's push pipeline. Surfacing as a finding for operator awareness; will write a PROP if next 3 runs show same 100% fail-rate without retry evidence.

findings.description: CLAUDE.md `Monitoring Pipeline` table lists workspace-sync schedule as 'Every 4h', but actual cron is hourly (`0 * * * *`, last 09:03Z, next 10:03Z). Schedule was bumped 2026-04-25 per the dome-workspace-sync description in list_scheduled_tasks: 'Bumped to hourly 2026-04-25 — fewer agent runs lose effective work to FUSE↔git sync staleness when sync runs every hour vs every 4h.' tinker.md dispatcher table also says 'Every 4h' for workspace-sync. CLAUDE.md is read by every new session and every agent; an out-of-date schedule there can mislead any new contributor or any agent doing self-audit. Same drift exists in monitor/prompts/tinker.md.
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md line ~218 'dome-workspace-sync | Every 4h | Haiku | workspace-sync.md | Commits workspace-only files to git'. monitor/prompts/tinker.md line 22 'Workspace-sync | workspace-sync.md | Every 4h | pushes workspace-owned files → git'. Live cron from list_scheduled_tasks: dome-workspace-sync cronExpression='0 * * * *', schedule='At 3 minutes past the hour, every hour, every day'. dome-workspace-sync.description literally documents the bump.

suggested_fix.replace: workspace-sync row schedule column 'Hourly (bumped from 4h on 2026-04-25 per drain-mode HNOTE; revert to 4h once open-issues drop below 50 and curmudgeon queue stays at 0 for ≥48h)'
suggested_fix.rationale: CLAUDE.md is git-owned and operator-curated, so tinker writes a recommendation rather than self-applying. tinker.md is owned by the prompts folder and is also git-owned (NEVER_PUSH per workspace-sync.md). Both edits should land via a clean-clone operator commit or via decider self-apply if the change is precondition-clear.

findings.description: PROP-009r2.verification_pattern was env-gated on `[ -n "${CLEAN_CLONE:-}" ]`. Yesterday's tinker noted the gate short-circuits to STILL_BROKEN whenever CLEAN_CLONE is unset, and explicitly said 'If still flagged on the 2026-04-26 run, tinker will apply it.' This is the 2026-04-26 run and it's the 4th consecutive STILL_BROKEN flag. Manually setting CLEAN_CLONE=1 confirms FIXED. The underlying state has been FIXED since 2026-04-18; the env-gate is a verification recipe bug, not a state regression.
findings.evidence: Default-env run of yesterday's pattern: STILL_BROKEN. CLEAN_CLONE=1 run of yesterday's pattern: FIXED. All six required substrings present: decider.md ('PROP-009 precondition' x2, 'prop-009-enforce.flag' x1, 'pq.history.length>200' x1), curmudgeon.md ('PROP-009 precondition' x1), build.js ("'monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag': 'git'," x1), workspace-sync.md ("'monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag'" x1).

suggested_fix.replace: Drop the env-gate; keep all six grep checks unchanged. Add verification_pattern_history block recording the prior pattern + reason for change.
suggested_fix.rationale: Conservative self-fix: change limited to the verification recipe, no semantic change to PROP-009r2 itself. This was explicitly authorized by yesterday's report ('If still flagged on the 2026-04-26 run, tinker will apply it'). Verification: bash -c <new_pattern> returns FIXED.

findings.fix_evidence: PROP-009r2.json verification_pattern updated; verification_pattern_history block added recording prior pattern and removal rationale; bash -c <new_pattern> returns FIXED in default env.

recommendations.description: When applying multi-step EXP sequences (like EXP-249/251/252 Phase B1/B3/B4), end each commit with `node build.js publish` so docs/index.html (and the FUSE workspace) stay in sync. Today's cd1ba07 EXP-249 Phase B1 commit pushed source data + generate-html.js but did not regenerate docs/index.html — operator-rescue path otherwise relies on workspace-sync's auto-regen at the next hour boundary. Adding `build.js publish` to the EXP-application playbook would prevent the 4-file FUSE staleness pattern observed here.

Decider's 100% 403 push-failure rate (8 of 8 last 16h) suggests the documented retry-on-403 wrapper is not effective. Either (a) the wrapper isn't firing in the actual push code path, or (b) the retry is also hitting 403 (transient pattern is longer than 30s). Actionable next step: instrument the wrapper to log retry_attempts: N in push-failure-*.json. Until then, universal-pusher rescue is masking the underlying issue at ~1h delay per cycle. Tinker can't audit decider internals; this is operator-only.

Decider should sync schedule_state in priority-queue.json on next run to match actual cron (60/240/120) and clear the 2026-04-17 'churn-and-burn' override note. Tinker cannot write this file. 9-day staleness, 3rd consecutive tinker carry-forward.
